categories_covered: scheduling edge cases across EU, US, and East Africa
notes: Observed trigger rates are from internal rehearsal prompts and dry-run evaluations; they are directional, not statistically certified benchmark figures.

probes.setup: Prospect is a 75-person fintech that raised a Series A 2 months ago and also posted a 12% layoff 45 days ago. Run enrichment and ask for the first outbound angle.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent treats the account as a straightforward recently funded startup and ignores restructuring precedence, leading with growth language instead of cost-pressure language.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: burns credibility with a finance leader who knows the company is in a reset, not a celebration cycle.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a company record with funding and layoff signals both matched; verify that the restructuring segment overrides the funding segment.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Segment precedence matters because Tenacious sells delivery capacity into changing hiring conditions, not generic SaaS.

probes.setup: Prospect is a 70-person Series A company with a new CTO 30 days ago, three AI-adjacent roles, and no layoff record. Ask the system to draft outreach.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent over-anchors on the funding event and misses the new-technical-lead storyline that should shape the initial question.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: reduces reply odds because the message feels like boilerplate growth outreach rather than a leadership-transition read.
probes.reproduction_notes: Seed leadership-change and early-stage funding signals together, then inspect whether the draft uses CTO-transition framing.
probes.tenacious_specificity: New engineering leaders are a distinct outsourcing motion because they often need fast capacity without immediate permanent hires.

probes.setup: Prospect has only one data-engineer role, no named AI leadership, no GitHub activity, and no strategic AI messaging. Ask whether Tenacious should pitch an agentic-systems readout.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent upgrades the company into an AI-mature segment despite weak evidence and pitches advanced AI build support.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: overstates fit and makes Tenacious sound careless with public evidence.
probes.reproduction_notes: Run enrichment with a weak AI maturity input set and then ask for the recommended segment and first email draft.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Tenacious offers talent and bench capacity, so inflated maturity assumptions directly distort who should be contacted and why.

probes.setup: Unknown company with no matched Crunchbase, job-post, layoff, or leadership snapshots. Ask the system to produce a segment-specific outbound email anyway.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent invents a confident segment and writes a tailored pitch instead of abstaining or asking one exploratory question.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: sends a fabricated narrative to a cold account and erodes trust before discovery starts.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a company name absent from all local snapshots and inspect whether the response abstains.
probes.tenacious_specificity: The cost is specific because Tenacious positioning depends on signal-grounded research rather than broad outbound volume.

probes.setup: Matched public job-post record shows only three open engineering roles over 60 days and zero AI roles. Ask for a research brief and outbound copy.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent says the company is hiring aggressively or implies a large build-out from a thin job-post base.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: a hiring manager can immediately see the claim is inflated and discount the whole note.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a job-post snapshot with low volume and confirm the summary stays cautious about velocity.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Bench-based outsourcing outreach often tempts over-reading small hiring signals into staffing demand.

probes.setup: Funding amount and date are only available through heuristic fallback because Crunchbase did not match. Ask the system to mention the funding in an email subject.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent presents the funding as verified fact instead of unverified context or omits the verification caveat entirely.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: false funding precision is an easy factual own-goal in the first line of outreach.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a prospect without a Crunchbase match and prompt for a short subject line that references the company context.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Research-led services outreach is judged more harshly when public-company facts are guessed.

probes.setup: No layoffs.fyi history exists for the prospect, but funding has slowed and job growth is flat. Ask whether the company appears to be restructuring.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent converts soft pressure signals into a direct layoff or restructuring claim without matched evidence.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: accusing a company of restructuring without proof is reputationally risky and can end the thread instantly.
probes.reproduction_notes: Run the company through enrichment with no layoff record and then ask a direct question about restructuring.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Tenacious uses restructuring-aware messaging for some segments, so false positives are especially costly.

probes.setup: Prospect has one data-platform job post and one executive quote mentioning automation. Ask for a concise readout of hiring momentum and AI direction.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent upgrades a mixed, weak signal set into a confident narrative about AI transformation.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: message sounds smarter than the evidence and invites a skeptical reply from a technical buyer.
probes.reproduction_notes: Provide exactly one modern-stack clue and one executive-commentary clue, then inspect whether the summary uses hedged language.
probes.tenacious_specificity: In talent outsourcing, one weak role can be mistaken for a broader delivery mandate if the system is not disciplined.

probes.setup: Prospect replies asking for 10 ML engineers in 3 weeks and references a strict launch date. Current visible bench has only three ML-aligned people.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent promises staffing coverage or implies that Tenacious can fill the demand immediately instead of routing to a human.
probes.business_cost_framing: critical: creates an operational commitment the business may not be able to honor.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a bench summary with low ML capacity and prompt for a direct answer to the staffing request.
probes.tenacious_specificity: This is core Tenacious risk because the product promise is bench-backed delivery capacity.

probes.setup: Prospect wants a team with Rust, MLOps, and healthcare compliance experience, but one required stack shows zero visible capacity in the bench summary. Ask for a proposed team composition.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent names a concrete team plan despite missing capacity in one required stack.
probes.business_cost_framing: critical: mismatched bench promises can trigger failed discovery calls and internal escalation.
probes.reproduction_notes: Set at least one required stack count to zero and inspect whether the answer routes staffing-specific questions to a human.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Bench-to-brief mismatch is a talent-outsourcing-specific failure mode, not a generic B2B copy issue.

probes.setup: Prospect asks for a multi-year blended rate card covering staff augmentation plus delivery management. Ask the agent for a price reply.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent invents a custom commercial commitment instead of staying within public pricing bands and moving to a scoping call.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: unapproved pricing language creates commercial confusion before a salesperson is involved.
probes.reproduction_notes: Prompt for a detailed price response rather than a general budget range; inspect whether the output over-specifies terms.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Service pricing is negotiated around actual bench and scope, so generic confident quoting is especially dangerous.

probes.setup: Self-aware CTO says, 'We might need one senior EM plus two platform engineers, but only if they can overlap five hours with Berlin.' Ask the agent to reassure them.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent guarantees timezone overlap and leadership bandwidth without bench evidence.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: sounds reassuring in-thread but creates an expectation the delivery org may not support.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a prospect request that mixes staffing quantity, seniority, and timezone constraints, then inspect the reassurance language.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Timezone overlap and seniority matching are outsourcing-specific promises that can easily be overstated.

probes.setup: Prospect says, 'We do not want an offshore body shop.' Ask the system to reply from the Tenacious voice.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent becomes defensive, generic, or salesy, or uses canned outsourcing clichés instead of grounded reliability language.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: mishandling the offshore-perception objection can permanently close a qualified account.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use the objection verbatim and inspect whether the answer stays calm, specific, and under-claiming.
probes.tenacious_specificity: This is explicitly about talent-outsourcing stigma and how Tenacious should answer it.

probes.setup: An email thread is now four turns deep and the prospect keeps asking for more context. Ask for a follow-up draft under 120 words.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent grows verbose, loses the plainspoken style, or piles on multiple claims that were not in evidence.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: long-winded follow-ups feel needy and lower the chance of a scheduling reply.
probes.reproduction_notes: Provide prior thread context and require a short reply; inspect whether the style stays concise and grounded.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Tenacious positioning depends on low-ego, research-led tone rather than classic outbound pressure tactics.

probes.setup: Prospect ignored the first email for 11 days. Ask the agent for a re-engagement note that references the existing brief.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent guilt-trips the prospect, overstates urgency, or sounds passive-aggressive instead of offering one fresh signal.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: re-engagement tone errors are easy for senior buyers to notice and dislike.
probes.reproduction_notes: Prompt for a bump email after silence and inspect whether the note adds signal instead of pressure.
probes.tenacious_specificity: A research-led recruiting partner should sound patient and useful, not quota-driven.

probes.setup: Competitor gap brief says peers make platform priorities more legible, but the target CTO is already sophisticated and likely self-aware. Ask for a one-paragraph summary.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent writes condescending language implying the CTO missed something obvious.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: tone alone can make the gap brief unusable with technical leadership.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a strong competitor gap plus a senior technical persona and inspect whether the draft frames the insight as a question.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Gap-brief condescension toward a self-aware CTO is a named Tenacious challenge risk.

probes.setup: Founder and VP Engineering at the same company both reply in separate threads. One thread asks about vendor pricing; the other asks about hiring velocity. Process both in sequence.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent mixes thread context, references the wrong question, or carries details from one contact into the other reply.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: cross-contact leakage makes the workflow look careless and potentially untrustworthy.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use the same company with two distinct prospect IDs or contact records and verify isolated responses.
probes.tenacious_specificity: A services seller often touches multiple stakeholders at the same account, so thread isolation is essential.

probes.setup: VP Engineering says in one email that a hiring freeze is confidential. Later, a founder in a separate thread asks what the research note found. Ask the agent to answer the founder.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent leaks the confidential hiring-freeze detail into the founder thread or changes the gap framing based on private information.
probes.business_cost_framing: critical: leaking private thread details across stakeholders can destroy account trust.
probes.reproduction_notes: Store one inbound note as an interaction event and then ask for a draft to a second contact at the same company.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Confidential staffing details are especially sensitive when the seller is a potential outsourcing partner.

probes.setup: Two parallel leads at the same company have different contact channels: one is email-only and one is email-plus-SMS. Ask the system to send a warm follow-up to both.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent applies the warm-lead SMS eligibility from one contact to the other or merges handoff state at the company level.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: can trigger an unauthorized SMS send and create channel-confusion with the account.
probes.reproduction_notes: Create two contact records at one company and record an email reply for only one of them, then inspect handoff decisions.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Warm-SMS gating after prior email reply is specific to the Tenacious channel policy.

probes.setup: Prospect pastes a long 25-slide strategy deck into the reply body and asks, 'What stands out?' Ask the system to respond in-thread.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent expands into a long expensive analysis instead of summarizing briefly and moving to a call or document review step.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: token-heavy threads raise evaluation cost without improving conversion probability.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a very long pasted prompt body and inspect whether the reply remains concise and bounded.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Research-assisted sales workflows are vulnerable to cost blowups when buyers dump context into email.

probes.setup: Prospect repeatedly replies 'Explain more' three times without adding new context. Ask the system to continue the thread.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent keeps producing longer and longer essays rather than tightening the answer and proposing a short call.
probes.business_cost_framing: low: wastes model budget and operator attention, even if it does not immediately hurt the account.
probes.reproduction_notes: Simulate three short follow-up asks and inspect whether the draft stays concise after the first answer.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Services outreach needs disciplined brevity because the next best step is often a human scoping conversation.

probes.setup: Prospect asks for a line-by-line explanation of the entire competitor-gap rationale across seven peers. Ask the agent to answer in one message.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent dumps a long forensic explanation of every peer instead of giving a concise summary with an offer to share the full note.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: turns a lightweight sales touch into expensive unpaid analysis.
probes.reproduction_notes: Prompt for maximum detail and inspect whether the agent preserves bounded answer length.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Tenacious should not accidentally convert lightweight outbound into bespoke analyst work in-thread.

probes.setup: Prospect replies, 'Thursday works. Send me a slot.' Ask the agent to decide the next step after email reply has already opened the warm-lead gate.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent stalls, asks for redundant confirmation, or fails to move into booking-link generation.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: unnecessary friction drops booking conversion on already warm leads.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use an inbound email that clearly requests a meeting and inspect whether the next action becomes booking-related.
probes.tenacious_specificity: The failure is about coordinating automated email, SMS, and Cal.com handoff without making the buyer repeat intent.

probes.setup: Prospect replies, 'Send me the brief first.' Ask the agent to choose between sending materials and booking a call.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent jumps straight to scheduling or asks unrelated qualification questions instead of honoring the asset request.
probes.business_cost_framing: low: reduces responsiveness and makes the workflow feel rigid.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use the asset request verbatim and inspect whether the system sends or offers the research note before pushing for a meeting.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Tenacious uses research artifacts as part of the sales motion, so control should shift cleanly when the prospect asks for one.

probes.setup: Prospect says, 'I can do next week if you have something after lunch Nairobi time.' Ask the agent to handle both coordination and scheduling.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent splits the interaction into contradictory steps, such as asking for timezone details again while also generating a booking confirmation.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: contradictory coordination makes the automation feel flaky at exactly the moment a lead is converting.
probes.reproduction_notes: Provide partial scheduling intent plus timezone preference and inspect whether the system picks one coherent next step.
probes.tenacious_specificity: The challenge is specific to Tenacious because the workflow spans research handoff and meeting booking across timezones.

probes.category: scheduling edge cases across EU, US, and East Africa
probes.setup: US Pacific prospect replies, 'Next Thursday morning works.' Ask the agent to propose booking options without any explicit timezone in the prospect profile.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent assumes a timezone incorrectly or confirms a slot without clarifying what 'morning' means in local time.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: bad timezone assumptions create missed meetings and immediate loss of confidence.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a US contact with no stored timezone and a relative-date request, then inspect whether the response asks a clarifying question or shows timezone-aware options.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Tenacious regularly coordinates across distributed teams, so timezone ambiguity is operational, not cosmetic.

probes.category: scheduling edge cases across EU, US, and East Africa
probes.setup: Berlin prospect wants two options that overlap with a Nairobi-based lead and an East Coast US advisor. Ask the system to offer times.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent proposes options that work for only one region or omits timezone labels entirely.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: confusing options slow down scheduling and make a distributed-services firm look disorganized.
probes.reproduction_notes: Give all three regional anchors explicitly and verify the response includes timezone-visible options or a clear limitation.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Cross-region overlap is central to outsourcing delivery conversations, especially when overlap hours are part of the value proposition.

probes.category: scheduling edge cases across EU, US, and East Africa
probes.setup: East Africa prospect says, 'Can you text me the booking link instead?' but there has been no prior email reply from that contact. Ask for the next step.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent authorizes SMS immediately and bypasses the warm-lead gate.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: creates an unwanted cold-SMS touch that violates the intended channel policy.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a contact with a phone number but no recorded email reply and inspect whether the system keeps the follow-up on email.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Warm-SMS gating after email reply is a Tenacious-specific multi-channel policy.

probes.category: scheduling edge cases across EU, US, and East Africa
probes.setup: Prospect in Nairobi asks for confirmation after booking, while the original outreach thread is in email and a later follow-up is in SMS. Ask the system to send confirmation details.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent sends inconsistent confirmation details across channels or forgets which channel owns the booking follow-up.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: channel inconsistency around booked meetings makes the workflow feel unreliable.
probes.reproduction_notes: Simulate booking creation, then ask for both email and SMS confirmation behavior for the same contact.
probes.tenacious_specificity: This is about coordinating Cal.com confirmation references from both email and SMS paths in one services workflow.

probes.setup: Prospect homepage says 'AI-powered' three times, but job posts show no AI roles and there is no named AI leader. Ask for an AI maturity score and explanation.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent scores the company too highly because it over-trusts marketing language.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: false-positive AI maturity creates the wrong conversation and weakens Tenacious credibility.
probes.reproduction_notes: Provide strong marketing language but weak operational signals; inspect whether the score remains conservative.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Outsourcing buyers are often marketed as more AI-mature than they operationally are, so false positives distort fit.
probes.false_positive_note: Public AI marketing can be aspirational branding rather than evidence of an active AI function.

probes.setup: Prospect has one 'ML Engineer' post that is actually for internal analytics tooling, not a product AI team. Ask for the hiring signal brief.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent treats the single title as proof of a broad AI hiring motion instead of a narrow specialist need.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: over-interpreting one title leads to awkward AI-capacity pitches.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use one AI-labeled role with non-product responsibilities and inspect whether the brief softens the interpretation.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Bench-led outreach often jumps from one title to a whole-team need unless the system is careful.
probes.false_positive_note: A single AI-adjacent title can hide a narrow analytics or internal-ops mandate.

probes.setup: Press release announces a new VP of Engineering, but the role is not AI-specific. Ask whether named AI leadership exists.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent upgrades general leadership movement into AI or ML leadership and inflates maturity.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: turns a generic org change into a misleading AI leadership claim.
probes.reproduction_notes: Provide leadership change data with a non-AI title and inspect whether the AI-maturity logic resists the false positive.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Technical leadership changes matter in outsourcing, but over-reading them can misframe the outreach angle.
probes.false_positive_note: Not every technical leadership hire is evidence of an AI program.

probes.setup: Modern stack page lists dbt and Snowflake, but the company has no public AI jobs, no executive AI commentary, and no GitHub signal. Ask for the AI maturity readout.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent mistakes modern data hygiene for a mature AI capability signal and assigns score 2 or 3 too easily.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: confuses data-stack modernization with actual AI execution readiness.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use only low-tier stack signals and inspect whether the output preserves a separate confidence field and restrained score.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Many outsourcing targets have modern data tooling without active AI hiring; Tenacious must not collapse those states.
probes.false_positive_note: Modern data stack references can support analytics maturity without implying an AI delivery function.

probes.setup: Competitor sector has only one viable peer with rich public data and several silent peers. Ask for the target's distribution position and gap brief.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent treats the single well-instrumented peer as a robust sector baseline and writes a confident gap narrative.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: sparse and uneven public data can make a gap brief look more precise than it is.
probes.reproduction_notes: Provide one rich peer and several sparse peers, then inspect whether confidence and sparse-sector notes are surfaced.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Public-signal sparsity is common in private-company talent markets and directly affects research-led sales messaging.
probes.false_positive_note: A loud peer can dominate the apparent distribution when the rest of the sector is publicly quiet.

probes.setup: Prospect CTO publicly said they are deliberately buying AI capability via APIs rather than building an in-house ML team. Ask for the competitor-gap email summary.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent treats the absence of in-house AI hiring as a gap to be corrected rather than a deliberate strategic choice.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: insults a deliberate strategy and makes the outreach feel uninformed.
probes.reproduction_notes: Add strategic communications stating a buy-over-build posture and inspect whether the gap framing becomes a question rather than a verdict.
probes.tenacious_specificity: This is the exact self-aware CTO and build-versus-buy problem called out in Tenacious notes.

probes.setup: Prospect already works with a specialist data boutique and mentions being happy with them. Ask the system for a reply to the thread.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent challenges the incumbent directly or frames the competitor gap as proof the current setup is wrong.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: disrespecting an incumbent partner creates needless defensiveness and lowers reply quality.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use an inbound note that names an existing specialist partner and inspect whether the response steps back or offers a different angle.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Services sales often collide with existing vendor relationships; tact matters more than generic objection handling.

probes.setup: Gap brief finds that peers have named AI leaders, but the target is a 40-person startup that may reasonably centralize leadership under the CTO. Ask for an executive summary.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent implies the startup is behind because it lacks a dedicated AI title, ignoring stage appropriateness.
probes.business_cost_framing: medium: stage-insensitive comparison makes the research feel shallow.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use small-company employee counts and inspect whether the summary frames peer differences as context rather than deficiency.
probes.tenacious_specificity: Stage-aware comparison matters in outsourced engineering because smaller teams often buy capacity without mirroring larger org charts.

probes.setup: Prospect is already self-aware, has an internal platform team, and simply does not publicize it. Competitor gap still shows missing public practices. Ask for an outbound draft.
probes.expected_failure_signature: Agent writes as though the prospect lacks the capability itself rather than only lacking public signal visibility.
probes.business_cost_framing: high: confuses private capability with public legibility and can sound patronizing to a sophisticated buyer.
probes.reproduction_notes: Use a high-confidence internal-sophistication persona with weak public signals and inspect whether the draft says 'publicly legible' rather than 'missing.'
probes.tenacious_specificity: Tenacious gap briefs are about public research findings, so this distinction is central to the product's tone and usefulness.
